problem_statement: Caption packages for six county public meetings are complete in the captioning system but never appear in the public archive after a service-identity migration. Other archive content continues to publish. Trace the broken cross-service path and identify a bounded correction using retained packages and policy simulation only; no public meeting page may be altered for experimentation.

observations.statement: The public archive freshness monitor alerted at 11:20Z when six meeting pages remained without captions more than two hours after their packages were marked ready.
The caption packager emitted package-ready for meeting 1842 with a valid object reference and correlation cap-20260709-1842.
The message gateway recorded an exact-principal denial for the same correlation 14 milliseconds later, before the publication router could acknowledge the package.
The router's correlation index contains no receive or reject span for cap-20260709-1842, and the archive sink contains no object derived from that correlation.
observations.provenance: router trace index and archive lineage index

observations.statement: The rendered principal-membership document contains caption-packager-v2 followed by a terminal space, while the service token and route declaration contain the same name without that space; membership comparison is exact.
observations.provenance: deployed policy bundle and signed service token

observations.statement: An offline policy evaluator denies all six retained v2 envelopes with the deployed bundle and allows them after removing only the terminal space; every unrelated route decision remains unchanged.
The same retained caption package, evaluated under the still-authorized v1 service identity and routed to a non-public capture sink, crosses the gateway and is accepted by the unchanged router.
Package signatures, WebVTT validation, object checksums, and meeting identifiers all pass independent validation for the six missing packages.
The caption packager and publication router revisions did not change during the identity migration, and the gateway delivered other topics without elevated latency or errors.
observations.provenance: deployment ledger and gateway topic health report
